session.user:
In each of these files, rename all occurrences of `plan_backend` to `pr_backend`, and rename `require_plan_backend` to `require_pr_backend` wherever it appears:
1. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/track_learn_evaluation.py` 2. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/post_workflow_started_comment.py` 3. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/push_session.py` 4. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/register_one_shot_plan.py`
This includes: - Imports - Parameter names - Variable names and attribute accesses - Type annotations - Comments
DO NOT rename: - External API field names - User-facing string messages
Read each file first, then apply all renames using the Edit tool.
session.assistant: I'll help you rename `plan_backend` to `pr_backend` and `require_plan_backend` to `require_pr_backend` in those files. Let me start by reading all four files to understand their contents.
session.tool_result:
1→"""Track learn evaluation completion on a plan. 2→ 3→This exec script posts a tracking comment to the plan and updates 4→the plan-header metadata block to record that learn evaluation was performed. 5→It replaces the tracking side-effect in `erk learn --no-interactive`. 6→ 7→Usage: 8→ erk exec track-learn-evaluation <plan-number> --session-id="..." 9→ 10→Output: 11→ JSON object with tracking result: 12→ { 13→ "success": true, 14→ "pr_number": 123, 15→ "tracked": true 16→ } 17→ 18→Exit Codes: 19→ 0: Success 20→ 1: Error (invalid plan, GitHub failure, etc.) 21→""" 22→ 23→import json 24→from dataclasses import asdict, dataclass 25→from datetime import UTC 26→from pathlib import Path 27→ 28→import click 29→ 30→from erk_shared.context.helpers import (
... [186 lines omitted] ...
39→from erk_shared.pr_store.types import PlanHeaderNotFoundError 52→class TrackLearnError: 53→ """Error result when tracking fails.""" 56→ error: str 122→ except PlanHeaderNotFoundError: 123→ error = TrackLearnError( 125→ error="no-metadata-block", 131→ click.echo(json.dumps(asdict(error)), err=True) 133→ except RuntimeError as e: 134→ error = TrackLearnError( 136→ error="github-api-failed", 137→ message=f"Failed to track learn evaluation on PR #{pr_number}: {e}", 139→ click.echo(json.dumps(asdict(error)), err=True) 171→ error = TrackLearnError( 173→ error="invalid-plan-identifier", 176→ click.echo(json.dumps(asdict(error))) 187→ error = TrackLearnError( 189→ error="no-plan-specified", 192→ click.echo(json.dumps(asdict(error)))

1→#!/usr/bin/env python3 2→"""Post a "workflow started" comment to a GitHub issue with YAML metadata block. 3→ 4→This command posts a structured comment to a GitHub issue indicating that a 5→GitHub Actions workflow has started. The comment includes a YAML metadata block 6→that can be parsed programmatically. 7→ 8→This replaces ~40 lines of bash heredoc template assembly in GitHub Actions workflows. 9→ 10→Usage: 11→ erk exec post-workflow-started-comment \\ 12→ --pr-number 123 \\ 13→ --branch-name my-feature-branch \\ 14→ --impl-pr-number 456 \\ 15→ --run-id 12345678 \\ 16→ --run-url https://github.com/owner/repo/actions/runs/12345678 \\ 17→ --repository owner/repo 18→ 19→Output: 20→ JSON object with success status 21→ 22→Exit Codes: 23→ 0: Success (comment posted) 24→ 1: Error (GitHub API failed) 25→ 26→Examples: 27→ $ erk exec post-workflow-started-comment \\ 28→ --pr-number 123 \\ 29→ --branch-name feat-auth \\ 30→ --impl-pr-number 456 \\
... [151 lines omitted] ...
62→class PostError: 63→ """Error result when comment posting fails.""" 66→ error: str 168→ except RuntimeError as e: 169→ result = PostError( 171→ error="github-api-failed",

1→"""Push a preprocessed session to the planned-pr-context branch with accumulation. 2→ 3→Preprocesses session JSONL to compressed XML and accumulates multiple sessions 4→on the same branch across lifecycle stages. 5→ 6→Usage: 7→ erk exec push-session \\ 8→ --session-file /path/to/session.jsonl \\ 9→ --session-id abc-123 \\ 10→ --stage planning \\ 11→ --source local \\ 12→ --plan-id 2521 13→ 14→Output: 15→ Structured JSON output with upload status: 16→ {"uploaded": true, "pr_number": 2521, "session_branch": "planned-pr-context/2521", 17→ "files": [...]} 18→ {"uploaded": false, "reason": "preprocessing_failed"} 19→ 20→Exit Codes: 21→ 0: Always (non-critical operation, graceful degradation) 22→""" 23→ 24→import json 25→import subprocess 26→import tempfile 27→from datetime import UTC 28→from pathlib import Path 29→from typing import Literal 30→
... [298 lines omitted] ...
63→ List of XML file paths on success, None on failure. 223→ click.echo(json.dumps({"uploaded": False, "reason": "preprocessing_failed"})) 304→ except RuntimeError:

Excellent! All the renames have been successfully applied. Let me create a summary of the changes made:
## Summary
I have successfully renamed `plan_backend` to `pr_backend` and `require_plan_backend` to `require_pr_backend` across all four files. Here are the details:
### File 1: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/track_learn_evaluation.py` - Line 33: `require_plan_backend` → `require_pr_backend` (import) - Line 160: `require_plan_backend(ctx)` → `require_pr_backend(ctx)` (function call)
### File 2: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/post_workflow_started_comment.py` - Line 46: `require_plan_backend` → `require_pr_backend` (import) - Line 147: `require_plan_backend(ctx)` → `require_pr_backend(ctx)` (function call)
### File 3: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/push_session.py` - Line 35: `require_plan_backend` → `require_pr_backend` (import) - Line 291: `require_plan_backend(ctx)` → `require_pr_backend(ctx)` (function call)
### File 4: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/register_one_shot_plan.py` - Line 17: `require_plan_backend` → `require_pr_backend` (import) - Line 42: `plan_backend = require_plan_backend(ctx)` → `pr_backend = require_pr_backend(ctx)` (variable name and function call) - Line 49: `plan_backend=plan_backend` → `pr_backend=pr_backend` (parameter in function call) - Line 89: `plan_backend.update_metadata(...)` → `pr_backend.update_metadata(...)` (method call)
All renames include imports, parameter names, variable names, attribute accesses, and function calls. No external API field names or user-facing messages were modified.
